clusters.title: Cabinet keeps $ 2.5 m Treasury breach off agenda

versions.title: Cabinet keeps $ 2.5 m Treasury breach off agenda
versions.deck: The Government said yesterday that neither President and Finance Minister Anura Kumara Dissanayake nor the Cabinet had discussed the reported $ 2.5 million Treasury cyber breach and internal investigation, leaving the matter to the Finance Ministry and external investigative agencies. clusters.title: $ 625,000 payment to US Post goes missing

versions.title: $ 625,000 payment to US Post goes missing
versions.deck: Cabinet Spokesman and Mass Media Minister Dr. Nalinda Jayatissa yesterday said investigations were underway into a $ 625,000 payment remitted from Sri Lanka to the United States Postal Service, after US authorities informed local officials that the funds had not been received. versions.deck: LB Finance PLC has secured a $15 million senior debt facility from Enabling Qapital Ltd. (EQ), a Switzerland-based impact asset manager, marking another significant step in the Company’s strategic funding journey and reinforcing its commitment to advancing inclusive finance in Sri Lanka. clusters.title: Court imposes travel bans on five officials over $ 2.5 m Treasury cyber breach

versions.title: Court imposes travel bans on five officials over $ 2.5 m Treasury cyber breach
versions.deck: The Colombo Fort Magistrate’s Court yesterday imposed overseas travel bans on five officials in connection with the alleged $ 2.5 million cyber breach involving Sri Lanka Treasury funds, following a request by the Criminal Investigation Department (CID). versions.title: Mideast war: Can the crisis be turned into an opportunity?

clusters.title: Sri Lanka’s missing Balance Sheet

versions.title: Sri Lanka’s missing Balance Sheet
versions.deck: Sri Lanka’s crisis is often described in familiar terms: excessive borrowing, weak fiscal discipline and unsustainable debt. This diagnosis is not wrong. But it is incomplete. At its core, the crisis reflects a deeper issue in how sovereign financial positions are assessed and managed. Fiscal analysis remains overwhelmingly focused on flows – deficits, debt servicing and financing needs – while the stock of assets underpinning the public sector

clusters.title: Energy supplies are crashing: What must Sri Lanka do?

versions.title: Energy supplies are crashing: What must Sri Lanka do?
versions.deck: According to Kpler, a global business intelligence company, seaborne crude and condensate exports were down 6.9 million bpd in March compared to the previous month, and are down another 1.8 million bpd through 20 April. This leads to the conclusion that loadings are likely to be down 8.8 million bpd from prewar levels. versions.title: Choosing disaster over prevention and mitigation: Wilful blindness or short-sighted idiocy?
versions.deck: Sri Lanka has been a serial victim of a brutal cocktail of natural and man-made macro disasters in recent times. These twin forces have not only claimed thousands of lives but have systematically crippled the country’s economic foundation, exposing a dangerous void in proactive national planning. versions.title: Govt. flags negligence and vulnerability in Treasury payments after $ 2.5 m theft
versions.deck: Industry and Entrepreneurship Development Deputy Minister Chathuranga Abeysinghe has pointed to “negligence and vulnerability in Treasury payments” following the $ 2.5 million cyber breach linked to a Government debt transaction, framing the incident as part of broader systemic weaknesses in public sector processes.
